perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise defined, capitalised terms, singular or plural, used in this Amendment, shall have the same meaning ascribed thereto in the GMA.
perturbation.changed_text: Capitalized terms used in this Amendment shall be interpreted based on common industry practice.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to the GMA for definitions, this change creates ambiguity about how capitalized terms are to be interpreted. While the document will still be utilizing terms that have already been defined on the GMA, that section has been removed. This is an in-text contradiction because Section 6.2 states that the GMA shall prevail when there is inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case of any inconsistency between the terms of the GMA and this Amendment regarding the subject-matter, the latter shall prevail. To the extent not inconsistent with this Amendment, all terms and conditions of the GMA shall remain valid and binding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the GMA as the primary source of definition for capitalized terms, instead relying on 'common industry practice.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the GMA prevails in cases of inconsistency. By changing the defined terminology, the document creates a situation where the GMA (Original Text) would prevail over the Amendment (Changed Text) despite the opposite intention from the contract (Contradicted Text).

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall enter into force on the date of its signature by both Parties and, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by the Parties through a subsequent amendment to the GMA, shall remain in force for the term of the referenced GMA.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall enter into force on the date of its signature by both Parties and shall remain in force for a period of five (5) years.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by the Parties through a subsequent amendment to the GMA, shall remain in force for the term of the referenced GMA,' the amendment now implies that the agreement is valid for only 5 years. This contrasts with Clause 6.2, which states that the GMA's terms and conditions remain valid and binding to the extent not inconsistent with the Amendment, creating potential confusion on the period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case of any inconsistency between the terms of the GMA and this Amendment regarding the subject-matter, the latter shall prevail. To the extent not inconsistent with this Amendment, all terms and conditions of the GMA shall remain valid and binding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies a fixed 5-year term for the amendment, directly contradicting the original intention for the amendment to remain in force for the term of the GMA, as stated in the original text. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states that the GMA's terms remain valid to the extent not inconsistent with the amendment. The fixed 5-year term is inconsistent with the GMA's original term if it exceeds 5 years, creating a contradiction related to the agreement's duration. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition because removing 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by the Parties through a subsequent amendment to the GMA, shall remain in force for the term of the referenced GMA,' leads to contradiction with other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2.1 Subject to sub-Clause 5.2.5 below and in the event the Representatives (i) fail to create such committee or (ii) do not agree on an amicable settlement within [*****] from the date the committee referred to in this sub-Clause 5.2 has been created or such longer period as may be agreed upon in writing by the Representatives (the "Amicable Settlement Period"), the Dispute shall be exclusively and finally settled under the Rules and Conciliation of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce (the "ICC") by an arbitral tribunal composed of three (3) arbitrators; each Party shall then appoint one (1) arbitrator within [*****]from the last day of the Amicable Settlement Period and the third arbitrator, who will act as President, will be appointed by the other two (2) arbitrators. In case the two (2) arbitrators appointed by the Parties do not agree on this choice with [*****] from the date the last arbitrator is appointed, the third arbitrator will be appointed by the ICC Court.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2.1 In the event the Representatives do not agree on an amicable settlement, the Dispute shall be settled by binding arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation omits key details about the arbitration process, including the specific rules of the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC), the number of arbitrators, and the process for appointing them. By deleting the clause referring to the ICC Rules and Conciliation of Arbitration, and the detailed arbitrator appointment process, it contradicts the intention of Clause 5.2 which establishes a specific detailed procedure, and makes the process generic, and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: in the event of a dispute arising out of or relating to this Amendment, including without limitation disputes regarding the existence, validity or termination of this Amendment (a "Dispute"), either Party may notify such Dispute to the other through service of a written notice (the "Notice of Dispute"). The Parties shall make their reasonable endeavours to settle the Dispute amicably by a committee composed of one (1) management representative of each Party (the "Representatives"). Such committee shall be created by the Parties within [*****] from the date of receipt of the Notice of Dispute.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the detailed arbitration process outlined in the original text, including the ICC rules and the arbitrator appointment process. This omission directly contradicts the contradicted text, which establishes an amicable settlement process involving a committee of representatives. By making the dispute settlement process generic and lacking specific procedures, the change creates a strong contradiction, making the contract unenforceable as the parties will have no agreed upon process on how to arbitrate in case settlement is not reached.
